candidates.title: Gravestones decommissioned into folk-art museum collection
candidates.shift: A cemetery with no burials curates historic Alpine grave monuments purely as irreverent folk-art artifacts, severing markers from memorial function.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: signals a desacralization pathway where funerary objects migrate into heritage/entertainment economies — a template for how death markers could be repurposed elsewhere as burial customs shift.
candidates.evidence: Museumsfriedhof Kramsach holds authentic old grave monuments with irreverent rhymed epitaphs; nobody is buried there.

candidates.title: Private, unmarked mausoleum architecture in forest landscape
candidates.shift: A 1926 individual mausoleum built by a private citizen sits hidden in forest, outside institutional cemetery systems entirely.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: an early precedent for privatized, dispersed, non-institutional death architecture — relevant as future funerary practice fragments away from centralized cemeteries and religious institutions.
candidates.evidence: Klinger-Mausoleum built by Baron Hugo Klinger for his wife, tucked in forest near Kollmitzdörfl, Austria.

candidates.title: Geographic-center claim doubling as sacred/strategic site
candidates.shift: A hill claimed as the geographic center of the Iberian Peninsula accrues centuries of pilgrimage and strategic significance simultaneously.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: illustrates how symbolic cartographic claims (centers, midpoints) become politically and spiritually load-bearing — a small analog for future contested-geography disputes.
candidates.evidence: Cerro de los Ángeles long considered geographic center of Iberian Peninsula and historic pilgrimage sanctuary site.

candidates.title: LLM co-writing threads reportedly degrade after two weeks
candidates.shift: A community observation that AI-assisted prose quality softens as a chat/context thread ages over roughly two weeks.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: an unstudied failure mode of sustained human-AI creative collaboration — context fatigue or drift in long-running co-authorship, worth tracking as AI writing partnerships lengthen.
candidates.evidence: HN thread titled 'ChatBOT chapter thread is two weeks old. That's why the prose went soft.'

candidates.title: Oyster-habitat homage sculpture as ecological grief art
candidates.shift: Public garden sculptures shaped to mimic oyster habitats memorialize degraded marine ecosystems through whimsical art.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: a minor but genuine example of ecological-grief aesthetics entering mainstream public art, possibly foreshadowing more memorial art for vanishing marine species/habitats.
candidates.evidence: 'Drops of Rain' sculptures at Jardins d'Étretat designed to resemble oyster habitats.

candidates.title: Colonial charity-house burial reveals pre-colonial layer
candidates.shift: A Jesuit charity mansion in Puebla sits atop a burial site over 2,000 years old, discovered by 2010 excavation.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: peripheral-geography case where colonial-era heritage sites are forced to renegotiate identity once older indigenous layers surface — a pattern likely to recur globally.
candidates.evidence: Museo Casa del Mendrugo excavation uncovered burial site more than 2,000 years old in courtyard.

candidates.title: Indie post-quantum encryption bolted onto mainstream chat apps
candidates.shift: A single developer layers post-quantum E2EE onto Instagram, WhatsApp, and Telegram Web without platform cooperation.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: an early parasitic-security pattern where consumer PQ anxiety outruns platform-native upgrades, prompting unofficial overlay tools of uncertain trust/security quality.
candidates.evidence: Show HN post for Ekko, a post-quantum E2EE layer for major messaging platforms, 10 points/4 comments.

candidates.title: Quantifying Islamic civilizational renewal via 560 indicators
candidates.shift: A framework attempts to translate normative Islamic civilizational-renewal theology into an auditable, measurable 560-indicator governance architecture.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative cross-domain convergence of religious political theory and technocratic metrics — could presage alternative civilizational-index systems competing with Western development metrics.
candidates.evidence: UCTA Model II preprint describes 560-indicator architecture for measuring Islamic civilizational renewal.

candidates.title: China codifies extraterritorial jurisdiction over marine harm
candidates.shift: China's new Ecological and Environmental Code establishes jurisdiction over transboundary marine harm based on 'possibility of harm,' a precautionary trigger.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative reframing of sovereignty: a precautionary, possibility-based jurisdictional trigger could set precedent for unilateral environmental claims reshaping maritime law disputes elsewhere.
candidates.evidence: Article 3 of China's Ecological and Environmental Code establishes extraterritorial jurisdiction over transboundary marine harm.

candidates.title: No constant-size post-quantum quorum signature exists yet
candidates.shift: Research confirms an absence: no post-quantum threshold/multi-signature scheme achieves constant-size certificates, unlike classical quorum signatures.
candidates.ai_read: Tentative: this absence is a scaling bottleneck for future quantum-safe distributed consensus (blockchains, BFT systems) — worth watching as a structural gap rather than a solved problem.
candidates.evidence: arXiv paper states no constant-size post-quantum quorum signature is known; all candidates grow with signer count.

candidates.title: NIST-driven push toward practical multi-party post-quantum signatures
candidates.shift: Practical threshold post-quantum signature schemes (Threshold MAYO) are being engineered for high-assurance, multi-party deployments under NIST's MPTC initiative.
candidates.ai_read: Trend: genuine infrastructure hardening ahead of the quantum threat, though still framed with some hype around urgency; worth tracking as adoption baseline for critical systems.
candidates.evidence: Paper presents practical t-out-of-n threshold MAYO variant referencing NIST's Multi-Party Cryptography initiative.

candidates.title: Muralism scale claims as urban prestige competition
candidates.shift: A newly unveiled mural in Madrid claims the title of largest in Spain, continuing an escalating civic-prestige contest around monumental public art.
candidates.ai_read: Trend: muralism increasingly operates as a quantified superlative economy (biggest/largest), paralleling other infrastructure prestige races — mostly established pattern, low novelty.
candidates.evidence: Muse of Vistalegre mural now holds title of largest mural in Spain, on former bullring arena.

candidates.title: Unmarked ordinary buildings as informal celebrity pilgrimage sites
candidates.shift: An unremarkable Berlin residential building becomes an informal cultural pilgrimage site tied to David Bowie, absent formal museification.
candidates.ai_read: Trend: celebrity heritage attaches to mundane infrastructure through informal cultural memory rather than institutional preservation — established but useful heritage-tourism baseline.
candidates.evidence: House at Hauptstraße 155, Berlin, described as 'nothing special' yet culturally significant as Bowie's former residence.

candidates.title: Ancient indigenous route persists as modern footbridge chokepoint
candidates.shift: A multi-century travel corridor used successively by Native Americans, trappers, and settlers converges into a modern footbridge infrastructure node.
candidates.ai_read: Trend: illustrates persistence of geographic chokepoints across eras despite technological change — established pattern, low novelty but useful as deep-time infrastructure baseline.
candidates.evidence: Umtanum Creek Footbridge sits on Yakima Canyon route used for thousands of years by Native Americans then settlers.

candidates.title: Folk musician statuary as regional identity anchor
candidates.shift: A bronze statue honors an 18th-century Scottish fiddler as a permanent civic identity marker tied to traditional music heritage.
candidates.ai_read: Trend: established pattern of folk-music revival intersecting with civic monument-building; low novelty but a steady baseline for cultural-heritage branding via music figures.
candidates.evidence: Niel Gow Statue in Dunkeld honors the 'father of Scottish fiddle music' as local landmark.

candidates.title: Aviation disaster memorial persists as enduring coastal ritual site
candidates.shift: A roadside memorial for a 1998 plane crash continues functioning as a grief/pilgrimage site decades later near the crash location.
candidates.ai_read: Trend: established disaster-memorial pattern; useful baseline for understanding how transportation-disaster memorials embed into landscape and endure across generations.
candidates.evidence: Swissair Flight 111 Memorial near Peggy's Cove, Nova Scotia, marking 1998 crash that killed 229.

candidates.title: Oversized toy iconography as small-town branding anchor
candidates.shift: A municipal park built around giant teddy bear sculptures anchors local identity and tourism branding.
candidates.ai_read: Trend: low-novelty but persistent town-branding-via-oddity economy; a steady baseline example of whimsical monument tourism rather than an emerging shift.
candidates.evidence: Teddy Bear Park in Stillwater, Minnesota, self-styled 'Birthplace of Minnesota.'
